mnemonics.title: Recoverability Levels
mnemonics.content: Think of a pyramid: TOP = Strict (most restrictions), MIDDLE = Cascadeless, BOTTOM = Recoverable (least restrictions). Like security levels: Strict = Maximum security, Cascadeless = Medium security, Recoverable = Basic security.

mnemonics.title: Dirty Read Prevention
mnemonics.content: Never read what hasn't been committed - like never trust someone's word until it's in writing (committed). Uncommitted = verbal promise, Committed = signed contract.

mnemonics.title: Conflict Detection
mnemonics.content: R-R are friends (no conflict), W-W are rivals (conflict), R-W and W-R are enemies (conflict). Remember: Any write involvement means conflict!

mnemonics.title: Serializability Testing
mnemonics.content: Precedence Graph: Draw arrows from Transaction A to B when A's operation comes first and conflicts with B's later operation. Cycle = trouble, No cycle = secure!

acronyms.usage: Four essential properties of database transactions
One transaction reads, another writes same data - conflicting operations
One writes, another reads same data - conflicting operations
Two transactions write same data - conflicting operations
Actually non-conflicting - both read same data, no problem

visualTips.visualization: Draw three concentric circles: Inner = Strict (most restrictive), Middle = Cascadeless, Outer = Recoverable. Strict is inside Cascadeless inside Recoverable.
For each pair of transactions with conflicting operations, draw arrow from earlier transaction to later transaction. Then check for cycles using DFS or visual inspection.
Look for pattern: Transaction A writes/commits, Transaction B reads from A, then either commits or aborts BEFORE A commits. If B commits first with uncommitted read = dirty read = NOT recoverable!
Create a table with transactions as rows, data items as columns. Mark each R/W operation. Any cell with both R and W from DIFFERENT transactions = conflict!

analogies.analogy: Like a bank transfer - you don't credit the recipient's account until you've debited (written) from sender's account. If debit fails (aborts), credit never happens (recoverable).
Like reading a published newspaper, not a draft. You only act on information that's正式 (committed), not preliminary drafts that might change.
Like a legal document requiring all signatures before anyone can act. No one can use or modify the document until everyone has signed (committed/aborted).
Like multiple people cooking in a kitchen - serializability ensures the final dish tastes as if one person cooked everything in sequence, not a chaotic mess.
Two people reaching for the same coffee mug at the same time - that's a conflict! But two people reading the same menu (read-read) is fine - no conflict.
